EQUAL CREDIT OPPORTUNITY ACT NOTICE – ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC
LOAN #6351710946 / ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC / 02/13/2024
This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to Tyler Martin by Rocket Mortgage, LLC in connection with loan number 6351710946 and the credit transaction concerning the property located at 95888 Gonzalez Hollow Apt. 988, South Richland, VA 13402. The federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act and its implementing regulation, Regulation B, prohibit creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on certain prohibited bases and confer upon you important rights with respect to the evaluation of your application, the disposition of your request for credit, and your access to the reasons underlying any action taken. Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to extending credit to all creditworthy applicants in a consistent, and lawful manner, and this disclosure summarizes the protections, obligations, and remedies that govern the credit transaction described above as of February 13, 2024.
1. PROHIBITED BASICS OF DISCRIMINATION
The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B make it unlawful for any creditor, including Rocket Mortgage, LLC, to discriminate against an applicant with respect to any aspect of a credit transaction on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or because you receive public assistance. These prohibited bases are commonly referred to as "protected characteristics." In evaluating an application, including the information received from all sources, Rocket Mortgage, LLC is required to make a credit decision based on credit-related factors only, without regard to these protected characteristics. Similarly, Rocket Mortgage, LLC is required to inform you of its decision on your application as part of the necessary evaluation process. The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B also prohibit creditors from discriminating against an applicant or borrower for the purpose of the credit requested, dependent upon whether the applicant has responded to an inquiry regarding their sex and marital status, or being harassed or intimidating in regard to any aspect of the credit transaction requested. Each of these prohibitions applies at every stage of the transaction associated with loan number 6351710946.

1. TREATMENT OF MARITAL STATUS  
Consistent with Regulation B, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will not refuse to extend credit, nor will it terminate or unfavorably alter the terms of an existing extension of credit, solely on the basis of the marital status of Tyler Martin, except where such consideration is expressly permitted or required by applicable law. You may apply for credit in your own name, and a creditor may not require the signature of a spouse or other person, other than a joint applicant, on any credit instrument if you qualify under the creditor's standards of creditworthiness for the amount and terms of the credit requested.  

Where the value of jointly owned property, or property located in a community property jurisdiction, is relied upon to satisfy the creditor's standards, additional signatures may be required to the extent necessary to make the collateral available to satisfy the debt in the event of default. For marital status and only to the extent reasonably necessary to protect the creditor's legitimate interest in the collateral securing loan number 8535179046.  

If you are married, you have the right to have credit history that reflects accounts you and your spouse use or for which you both are liable reported in the name of each spouse. Upon request, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will furnish independent credit reporting agencies in a manner that enables Tyler Martin to establish an independent credit history, subject to the accuracy of the underlying account records and the requirements of applicable law.  

3. NOTIFICATION OF ACTION TAKEN  
Regulation B requires Rocket Mortgage, LLC to notify Tyler Martin of the action taken on an application for credit within the time periods prescribed by law, generally within 30 days of receiving a completed application concerning the credit requested. The action taken may consist of an approval, a counter-offer to provide a different amount, terms, or a denial. If your application is in incomplete and does not expressly indicate the action taken, Rocket Mortgage, LLC may treat that application as withdrawn.  

If an application is incomplete regarding information that Tyler Martin can complete, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will notify Tyler Martin of the additional information needed, providing a reasonable period of time in which to furnish it, and that failure to furnish the information may result in denial of the application. The period allowed will be for no less than 30 days as outlined in loan number 8535179046.  

A notice of action taken will be in the form that you may retain, and it will identify Rocket Mortgage, LLC as the creditor, as well as the reasons for the action taken and any additional reasons required under the Fair Housing Act or other consumer-protection statutes applicable to the transaction.  

4. RIGHT TO A STATEMENT OF REASONS  
When Rocket Mortgage, LLC takes adverse action on an application for credit, or on an existing account, you have the right under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to a statement of the specific reasons for that action.

action. Adverse action includes a denial or revocation of credit, a change in the terms of an existing account that is not favorable to Tyler Martin and that is not applied to or substantially all of a class of the creditor's accounts, and a refusal to grant credit in substantially the amount or on substantially the terms requested in an application.

The statement of specific reasons will either be provided automatically with the notice of adverse action or made available upon your timely request. If the reasons are not disclosed in the initial notice, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will include a statement of your right to request the reasons within sixty (60) days of the notification, together with the name, address, and telephone number of the person or office from which the reasons may be obtained. Upon a timely oral or written request, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will furnish the specific reasons in writing within a reasonable period of time, generally not exceeding thirty (30) days.

The reasons disclosed will be specific and will indicate the principal factors that influenced the action taken; statements that the action was based on internal standards or policies, or that the applicant failed to achieve a qualifying score, are not by themselves sufficient. Any statement of reasons furnished in connection with loan number 6835170946 will accurately reflect the actual basis upon which the credit decision was made.

5: ADVERSE ACTION AND CREDIT SCORING
If Rocket Mortgage, LLC bases its credit decision in whole or in part on information obtained from a consumer reporting agency, the notice of adverse action provided to Tyler Martin will identify the agency that furnished the report, including its name, address, and telephone number, and state that the agency did not make the decision and is unable to provide the reasons for it. You may further advise your right to obtain a free copy of your credit report from the reporting agency where the credit report was obtained, and you may wish to check to determine whether the credit reporting agency provides additional information regarding the specific entity that provided the report information.

If Rocket Mortgage, LLC obtains information from an outside source other than a consumer reporting agency in connection with the notice or decision regarding the adverse action, the notice will include the name, address, and phone number of the entity that provided the information used. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will respond to any request for information regarding any adverse finding established under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and Regulation B.

6: GOVERNMENT MONITORING INFORMATION
For applications to purchase or refinance a dwelling occupied or to be occupied as a principal residence, and for applications to Rocket Mortgage, LLC to request information regarding the applicant's ethnicity, race, and sex for the purpose of monitoring compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act,

the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act, and other fair-lending statutes. The furnishing of this information by Tyler Martin is voluntary, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC may not discriminate on the basis of this information, or on the basis of whether you choose to provide it, in any aspect of the credit transaction.

If you choose not to furnish the requested monitoring information, federal regulators may require Rocket Mortgage, LLC to note the information on the basis of visual observation. Your information may be used in connection with the development of statistical underwriting criteria and plays an part in the evaluation of the creditworthiness of Tyler Martin in decision to extend, or contribute the credit associated with loan number 6835179046.

Monitoring information collected by Rocket Mortgage, LLC is maintained, reported, and safeguarded in accordance with applicable federal and WV law and is segregated from the factors used in underwriting to the extent required. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will not use this information for any purpose inconsistent with the regulatory objectives for which it is gathered.

7. APPRAISALS AND VALUATIONS  
In connection with an application for credit to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will provide Tyler Martin with copies of all appraisals and other valuations developed in connection with this application, whether or not the credit is ultimately extended. Such copies will be furnished promptly upon completion, or within the period prescribed by Regulation B before the application is withdrawn, whichever is earlier, regardless of whether the application results in a transaction.

You may waive the timing requirement for receiving these copies in advance, provided that Rocket Mortgage, LLC retains a copy of the acknowledgment of this waiver signed by Tyler Martin indicating that this specific provision is being waived. You may also withdraw the waiver of your right to receive a copy of the appraisal and the application, which will require you to provide a written notice to Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

These appraisal and valuation rights apply to the property identified as 96888 Gonzalez Hollow Apt. 968, South Richland, WV 13402 and are intended to ensure that Tyler Martin is timely access to the appraisal or other valuations, whichever is issued. Any questions concerning the valuations requested in this application shall be directed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at the address indicated.

8. INFORMATION REQUESTS AND OPTIONAL DISCLOSURES  
In evaluating your application, Rocket Mortgage, LLC may request any other information that is reasonable, necessary, or appropriate to evaluate the application and the circumstances of Tyler Martin, including, but not limited to, your age, sex, color, religion, national origin, or sex or other protected class, for the limited Mortgage loan purpose authorized by federal law when such status bars on the creditor's rights and remedies regarding credit requested.

You are not required to disclose income derived from alimony, child support, or separate maintenance payments unless you wish to have that income considered in support of your application. Should you elect to disclose such income, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will give it the same consideration extended to other income to the extent that it is likely to be consistently received and otherwise meets the creditor’s standards. You may also choose to provide explanatory information regarding any aspect of your financial circumstances relevant to the transaction.

Any information furnished by Tyler Martin in connection with icann number 6835770946 will be used in a manner consistent with applicable privacy and consumer-protection laws. Rocket Mortgage, LLC maintains records of credit applications and related documentation for the retention periods required by Regulation B and other governing authority.

9. ADMINISTERING FEDERAL AGENCY

The federal agency that administers compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act with respect to Rocket Mortgage, LLC is identified in the notice of action taken or the adverse-action notice provided in connection with this transaction. Depending on the nature of the transaction, oversight authority may rest with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, a federal banking regulator, or another designated agency, and the applicable agency’s name and mailing address is identified in the relevant notice furnished to Tyler Martin.

If you believe that Rocket Mortgage, LLC has discriminated against you on a prohibited basis, or has otherwise violated your rights under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, you may file a complaint with the administering federal regulatory agency identified in the notice. You retain the right to bring a private lawsuit regardless of whether you choose to file a complaint with the federal agency. This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is provided to notify you that this document does not constitute a commitment to extend credit, and does not state the terms of any agreement governing the credit transaction associated with loan number 6835770946.

10. RECORD RETENTION AND RESERVATION OF RIGHTS

Rocket Mortgage, LLC retains applications, notices, and supporting documentation relating to the credit transaction identified above for the periods of time required by applicable federal and state laws. In addition, that record retention includes any notices provided to you, the decisions reflected in those records, and any correspondence with you. This retention is maintained for a period consistent with the requirements of applicable laws governing the credit transaction associated with loan number 6835770946. 

In addition to the foregoing, all rights and remedies, tin such cases, may be made available to Tyler Martin under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, the Fair Credit Reporting Act, the Fair Housing Act, or any other applicable federal or VI consumer-protection statute. To the extent any provision of this

disclosure is found to conflict with controlling law, the governing statute or regulation shall prevail, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

This notice is current as of February 13, 2024 and reflects the fair-lending obligations applicable to Rocket Mortgage, LLC as of that date. Rocket Mortgage, LLC reserves the right to update this disclosure to conform to changes in applicable law or regulatory guidance occurring during 2024 or thereafter, and any such revision will be applied prospectively in accordance with governing authority.

Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures

HOUSING PROVIDER: Rocket Mortgage, LLC  (NMLS# 3030) 

The Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (“ESIGN”) permits consumers to consent to the use of electronic records and signatures in lieu of paper documents and handwritten signatures. By consenting to the use of electronic records and signatures, you agree that electronic signatures will have the same legal effect as handwritten signatures, and you are foregoing your right to receive paper copies of documents that are provided electronically.

1. SCOPE OF YOUR CONSENT  
You are consenting to receive electronic documents for all transactions between you and Rocket Mortgage, LLC (“Rocket”) related to your mortgage loan application. This includes, but is not limited to, documents relating to the processing, underwriting, and closing of your loan, as well as disclosures and notices required by law.  

2. HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS  
To access electronic documents, you will need:  
- a computer or other electronic device with internet access,  
- an email address,  
- a web browser that supports secure encryption, and  
- software that enables you to view and print PDF files if you wish to retain copies of the documents.  

3. WITHDRAWAL OF CONSENT  
You may withdraw your consent to receive electronic documents at any time. To withdraw your consent, you must contact us at the following address: Rocket Mortgage, LLC, 1050 Woodward Avenue, Detroit, MI 48226, or by calling us at 1-800-ROSERATE (1-800-767-3728). If you withdraw your consent, we will provide you with paper copies of any documents that would have been provided to you electronically.  

4. CHANGES TO YOUR CONTACT INFORMATION  
You must provide us with your current email address to receive electronic documents and you must notify us of any changes to your email address. You can update your email address by contacting us at the address or phone number listed above.  

5. EFFECTIVE DATE  
Your consent to the use of electronic records and signatures is effective as of the date of your electronic signature on your mortgage loan application.  

Please confirm that you understand and agree to the terms of this consent.

1  ELECTRONIC SIGNATURES  This electronic signature agreement ("Agreement") is entered into by and between you and the company. 2  HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS  To use our services, you are required to have certain hardware and software technologies. 3  CONTINUING YOUR ABILITY TO ACCESS  Your ability to access the services may be affected by several factors.

1.  INTRODUCTION
The purpose of this document is to outline the policies and procedures for the operation of our organization. It serves as a guide for staff and stakeholders.

2.  STANDARDS OF CONDUCT
All employees are expected to adhere to the highest standards of conduct. This includes treating colleagues with respect and maintaining a professional work environment.

3.  CONFIDENTIALITY
Employees must maintain the confidentiality of sensitive information at all times, even after their employment ends.

4.  EMPLOYEE WELFARE
Ensuring a safe and healthy work environment is a priority. Regular training will be provided.

5.  REPORTING VIOLATIONS
Any violations of the standards must be reported to a supervisor or through the anonymous reporting system available.

6.  AMENDMENT TO THESE POLICIES
These policies can be amended based on organizational needs or changes in the law.

I. WITHDRAWING YOUR CONSENT
You may withdraw your consent at any time by notifying us via the contact information provided below. However, please note that withdrawal of consent may limit your access to certain services that rely on your consent. If you have any questions regarding the withdrawal process, please contact us.

II. LEGAL AND SYSTEMAL GUARANTEES (WARRANTY)
We warrant that the services provided comply with the relevant legal standards. This warranty represents a limited guarantee of performance, meaning the organization commits to meeting the expectations outlined herein. If the services do not meet these standards, we will respond in accordance with our customer service policy.

III. RESOLUTION, LIMITATION, AND LIABILITY
In the event of a dispute, we encourage you to reach out to us directly to seek a resolution. If we are unable to resolve your concern, you may pursue a formal complaint via the appropriate legal channels. However, our liability is limited to the fullest extent permitted by law, and we are not responsible for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the services.

ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC — NOTICE OF SERVICING TRANSFER LENDER Rocket Mortgage, LLC
Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing
LOAN NO. 6835170946
Rights DATE 02/13/2024
BORROWER Tyler Martin
Issued to tyler MartIn
NOTICE & DISCLOSURE
This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Tyler Martin in connection with loan number 6835170946 secured by the property
located at 95888 Gonzalez Hollow Apt. 988, South Richardland, VI 13402, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and its
implementing regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan, meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is being or may be
transferred. This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late-fee
protection period operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it carefully and retain it with your permanent loan
records.
§ 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest,
and any escrowed amounts, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential mortgage industry and reflects an
arrangement between the prior servicer and Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable federal
regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one
servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing
mortgage documents. The information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to specify the effective dates relevant to
that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations of Tyler Martin during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the
terms of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly modified, continue to govern your loan.
§ 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and
interest payments, managing any escrow account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying payments to your account,
issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The
servicer may or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and the right to service it are distinct interests that may be
held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which Tyler Martin directs payments and account questions changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The
interest rate, the unpaid principal balance, the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory note and security
instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom
you address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed your loan documents.
§ 3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Rocket Mortgage, LLC. The
prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC will
begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any
payment obligation has lapsed. Continue to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and contact Rocket Mortgage,
LLC at (471)505-8799 if you have questions regarding which entity should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.
§ 4. IDENTITY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER
Your new servicer is Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it on and
after the transfer date. The business address for Rocket Mortgage, LLC is 0367 Miller Shoal, Cynthiamouth, MP 85220, and its toll-free or designated telephone number
for borrower assistance is (471)505-8799. Representatives are generally available during normal business hours to answer questions about your account, your payment
options, your escrow account, and the transition of servicing. When you contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC, please reference loan number 6835170946 so that your account
can be located promptly.
Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to a seamless transition and to honoring the terms of your existing loan. The records, payment history, and escrow information
associated with your loan are being transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC so that it may administer the loan without interruption. If you have established automatic
payment arrangements, recurring electronic drafts, or bill-payment instructions with your prior servicer, you should confirm with Rocket Mortgage, LLC whether those
arrangements have carried over or whether you must re-establish them, as such arrangements do not always transfer automatically between servicers.
§ 5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Rocket Mortgage, LLC rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the
address designated by Rocket Mortgage, LLC for the receipt of payments, which will be set forth in your welcome correspondence and on your periodic billing statements;
general correspondence and account questions may be directed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 0367 Miller Shoal, Cynthiamouth, MP 85220. To ensure that your payments
are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number 6835170946 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and follow any specific
remittance instructions provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

Payments received by your prior servicer after the transfer date will ordinarily be forwarded to Rocket Mortgage, LLC, but you should not rely on such forwarding as a
routine method of payment. To avoid delays in the crediting of your account, send all payments due on or after the transfer date directly to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. If you
remit payment through a third-party bill-payment service or your own financial institution, promptly update the payee name, address, and account number on file with that
service so that your payments are routed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC for all installments coming due on and after the effective date of the transfer.
§ 6. SIXTY-DAY LATE FEE PROTECTION
Federal law provides important protection to borrowers during a transfer of servicing. During the sixty (60) day period beginning on the effective date of the transfer of
servicing, a payment received by your prior servicer on or before its due date, or within any applicable grace period, may not be treated as late by either servicer, and no
late fee or other penalty may be imposed on you, and no delinquency may be reported to any consumer reporting agency, solely because the payment was sent to the prior
servicer rather than to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This protection is intended to guard against penalties that could otherwise arise from confusion about where to send
payments during the transition.
To benefit from this protection, you must still make each payment in full and on time according to the schedule in your loan documents; the sixty (60) day window protects
you only against being penalized for sending an otherwise timely payment to the wrong servicer, and it does not excuse a payment that is missed, made late, or made for
less than the amount due. After the sixty (60) day period has elapsed, all payments must be made to Rocket Mortgage, LLC in accordance with your loan documents, and
standard late charges and credit-reporting practices will apply to any payment that is not timely received by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.
§ 7. YOUR CONTINUING PAYMENT OBLIGATION
The transfer of servicing does not affect, reduce, suspend, or otherwise alter your obligation to make timely payments under your promissory note and security instrument.
Tyler Martin remains fully responsible for paying all amounts due, including principal, interest, and any required escrow deposits, on or before each scheduled due date,
regardless of which entity is servicing the loan at the time a payment comes due. The amount of your monthly payment, the application of payments to your account, and
all other contractual terms continue to be governed by your existing loan documents except to the extent they may be modified in a separate writing signed by the
appropriate parties.
If at any time you are unable to make a scheduled payment, you should contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC promptly at (471)505-8799 to discuss the options that may be
available to you. Do not allow uncertainty arising from the servicing transfer to cause you to withhold or delay a payment, as the protections described in this Notice are
limited and do not relieve you of your underlying obligation to pay. Keeping your account current throughout and after the transition is the most reliable way to preserve
your standing under the loan and to avoid the accrual of late charges or other consequences of delinquency.
§ 8. ESCROW ACCOUNT AND INSURANCE
If your loan includes an escrow account for the payment of property taxes, hazard insurance premiums, mortgage insurance, or other charges, the administration of that
account will transfer to Rocket Mortgage, LLC along with the servicing of your loan. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will continue to collect escrow deposits as part of your
monthly payment and to disburse funds from the escrow account to pay covered items as they come due. Your escrow account balance, payment history, and any cushion
permitted by applicable law will be transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and an annual escrow account statement will be furnished to you in accordance with applicable
requirements.
You should review your insurance policies and tax bills to confirm that Rocket Mortgage, LLC is correctly identified, where applicable, as the party to receive notices, and
you should forward to Rocket Mortgage, LLC any tax bills or insurance notices that you receive directly. Maintaining required hazard and, if applicable, flood insurance on
the property located at 95888 Gonzalez Hollow Apt. 988, South Richardland, VI 13402 remains your obligation under the security instrument. If required coverage lapses,
Rocket Mortgage, LLC may obtain lender-placed insurance as permitted by your loan documents and applicable law, the cost of which may be charged to you and may be
more expensive and provide less coverage than a policy you obtain on your own.
§ 9. NOTICES OF ERROR AND INFORMATION REQUESTS
If you believe an error has occurred in the servicing of your loan, or if you need information about your loan, you have the right to submit a written notice of error or a
written request for information to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. To be effective, such a notice or request should be sent in writing to the address that Rocket Mortgage, LLC
designates for the receipt of error notices and information requests, which may differ from the address used for payments; that designated address will be identified in your
statements and welcome materials, with general correspondence directed to 0367 Miller Shoal, Cynthiamouth, MP 85220. Your written communication should include your
name, loan number 6835170946, and sufficient detail to enable Rocket Mortgage, LLC to identify the account and the specific error or information at issue.
Upon receiving a qualifying written notice of error or information request, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will provide a written acknowledgment within the time required by
applicable law and will conduct a reasonable investigation or provide the requested information, or an explanation of why the information is unavailable, within the
applicable response period. While a covered error notice is being investigated, Rocket Mortgage, LLC may not furnish adverse information to a consumer reporting agency
regarding any payment that is the subject of the notice, to the extent required by law. Telephone calls to customer service may help resolve routine questions, but to invoke
these specific statutory protections you must submit your notice or request in writing.
§ 10. COMMUNICATIONS AND ACCOUNT INFORMATION
On and after the transfer date, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will send your periodic billing statements, escrow statements, year-end tax and interest summaries, and other
required disclosures relating to the servicing of your loan. You should expect to receive a welcome package or similar correspondence from Rocket Mortgage, LLC that
confirms your account number, payment address, due date, and the methods available for making payments and obtaining account information. Please review all such
communications carefully, retain them with your records, and notify Rocket Mortgage, LLC promptly of any discrepancy between the information shown and your
understanding of the terms of your loan.
To help ensure that you receive important notices without interruption, keep your mailing address, telephone number, and any email address on file with Rocket Mortgage,
LLC current at all times. If you have authorized electronic delivery of statements or other documents, confirm that your electronic delivery preferences have been carried
over to Rocket Mortgage, LLC or re-establish them as necessary. Should you have any question about the authenticity of a communication that appears to come from
Rocket Mortgage, LLC, you may verify it by contacting Rocket Mortgage, LLC directly at (471)505-8799 before providing any personal or financial information in
response.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

§ 11. EFFECT ON LOAN TERMS AND LEGAL RIGHTS
Except for the change in the identity of the servicer and the place to which payments and correspondence are directed, the transfer of servicing has no effect on the terms or
conditions of your mortgage loan other than those directly related to the servicing of the loan. Your promissory note, your security instrument, and any riders, addenda, or
modifications thereto remain binding according to their terms. The transfer does not constitute a refinancing, a novation, or a waiver of any right or remedy available to the
holder of the loan or to Tyler Martin, and it does not enlarge or diminish any defense or claim that either party may otherwise possess.
This Notice is provided for informational purposes and to satisfy applicable disclosure obligations; it is not legal or financial advice. If you have questions about how the
transfer of servicing affects your particular circumstances, or about your rights and obligations under federal or VI law, you may wish to consult an attorney or a qualified
housing counselor. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will administer your loan in accordance with the loan documents and applicable law, and it will make available to you, upon
reasonable request, information necessary to understand the status and handling of your account following the transfer.

cells.text:
ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC — NOTICE OF SERVICING TRANSFER
Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing
Rights
Issued to tyler MartIn

LENDER Rocket Mortgage, LLC
LOAN NO. 6835170946
DATE 02/13/2024
BORROWER Tyler Martin

This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Tyler Martin in connection with loan number 6835170946 secured by the property
located at 95888 Gonzalez Hollow Apt. 988, South Richardland, VI 13402, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and its
implementing regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan, meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is being or may be
transferred. This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late-fee
protection period operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it carefully and retain it with your permanent loan
records.
§ 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest,
and any escrowed amounts, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential mortgage industry and reflects an
arrangement between the prior servicer and Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable federal
regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one
servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing
mortgage documents. The information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to specify the effective dates relevant to
that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations of Tyler Martin during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the
terms of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly modified, continue to govern your loan.
§ 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and
interest payments, managing any escrow account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying payments to your account,
issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The
servicer may or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and the right to service it are distinct interests that may be
held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which Tyler Martin directs payments and account questions changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The
interest rate, the unpaid principal balance, the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory note and security
instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom
you address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed your loan documents.
§ 3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Rocket Mortgage, LLC. The
prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC will
begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any
payment obligation has lapsed. Continue to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and contact Rocket Mortgage,
LLC at (471)505-8799 if you have questions regarding which entity should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.
§ 4. IDENTITY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER
Your new servicer is Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it on and
after the transfer date. The business address for Rocket Mortgage, LLC is 0367 Miller Shoal, Cynthiamouth, MP 85220, and its toll-free or designated telephone number
for borrower assistance is (471)505-8799. Representatives are generally available during normal business hours to answer questions about your account, your payment
options, your escrow account, and the transition of servicing. When you contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC, please reference loan number 6835170946 so that your account
can be located promptly.
Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to a seamless transition and to honoring the terms of your existing loan. The records, payment history, and escrow information
associated with your loan are being transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC so that it may administer the loan without interruption. If you have established automatic
payment arrangements, recurring electronic drafts, or bill-payment instructions with your prior servicer, you should confirm with Rocket Mortgage, LLC whether those
arrangements have carried over or whether you must re-establish them, as such arrangements do not always transfer automatically between servicers.
§ 5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Rocket Mortgage, LLC rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the
address designated by Rocket Mortgage, LLC for the receipt of payments, which will be set forth in your welcome correspondence and on your periodic billing statements;
general correspondence and account questions may be directed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 0367 Miller Shoal, Cynthiamouth, MP 85220. To ensure that your payments
are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number 6835170946 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and follow any specific
remittance instructions provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

Hall, Baxter and James
94239 Brown Extension
South Danielhaven, NC 43901
Employer EIN: 57-9664739
